mainSource.description: Main quests are chained to cover Manual QA skills from Lv1 to Lv3.

main.title: Understand how QA fits into SDLC
main.description: Study the software testing lifecycle and write how QA work changes before, during, and after a release.

main.title: Write a clean test case set
main.description: Choose a public app flow and write test cases with preconditions, steps, expected results, and priority.

main.title: Run a smoke test checklist
main.description: Build and execute a smoke test checklist for login, navigation, one create flow, one edit flow, and one error case.

main.title: Plan a regression pass
main.description: Map an app feature into affected areas and write a regression checklist that protects old behavior after a change.

main.title: Create a bug report portfolio
main.description: Find five real or practice bugs and document reproduction steps, expected behavior, actual behavior, severity, and evidence.

main.title: Run a release-style QA review
main.description: Combine smoke, regression, bug reporting, and test case documentation into one release readiness report for a small app.

daily.title: Write three test cases for a daily app
daily.description: Pick an app you used today and write three test cases for one common flow.

daily.title: Practice one bug report
daily.description: Write one bug report from a real app, demo app, or remembered issue. Include steps, expected result, actual result, and evidence.

links.label: How to write a bug report

daily.title: Run one smoke checklist
daily.description: Choose any website and run a five-item smoke checklist covering load, navigation, login/search, one action, and error feedback.

daily.title: Compare functional and regression testing
daily.description: Read a short testing reference and write when you would run functional testing versus regression testing.
